title: Review & Setlist: ‘We are foreign imports, so we have paid tariffs.’
author: Marc Hirsh
bullets: The Hives performed at the House of Blues with elaborate stage antics and a high-energy setlist.
Frontman Howlin' Pelle Almqvist engaged the audience with charismatic showmanship and interactive moments.
Australian band The Chats opened with a spirited, punk-influenced performance before The Hives took the stage.

title: Cancer couldn’t stop her. Now she’s running the Boston Marathon again.
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Stephanie McHugh, 42, from Braintree, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for the New England Patriots Foundation.
Diagnosed with Stage 3 breast cancer in 2023, McHugh overcame treatment challenges to return to running.
Inspired by late friend Susan Hurley, McHugh is determined to complete her third Boston Marathon.

title: Markey presses Meta on facial recognition risks in smart glasses
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Senators, led by Ed Markey, warn Meta of privacy risks from facial recognition in smart glasses.
Lawmakers demand Meta clarify data collection and user consent policies by April 6.
Concerns arise over potential use of smart glasses to surveil political rallies and protests.

title: Debate in Quincy over funding for new firefighting gear after current gear tests positive for PFAS
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Quincy City Council delayed a vote on a $2.65 million plan for new PFAS-free firefighter gear.
Current gear used by Quincy firefighters tested positive for PFAS, linked to health risks like cancer.
Firefighters expressed frustration over the delay, citing ongoing health risks and potential legal actions.

title: The battle over the aging Attleboro Zoo is getting wild
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Mayor Cathleen DeSimone proposes transforming Capron Park Zoo into a nature reserve to address rising costs and needed $57 million repairs.
The proposed plan would focus on native wildlife conservation, reducing long-term costs, and potentially providing free or reduced admissions.
Capron Park Zoo, opened in 1937, currently houses over 100 animals; the transition team is expected to form in July.

title: What does it mean to have a partner?
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Abigail Brown and Colin Wong held a last-minute microwedding on Jan. 26, 2026, at Dearly Studios in Somerville.
FX's 'Love Story' highlights the nostalgia for pre-digital communication methods in relationships during the 1990s.
The Love Letters newsletter explores the evolving use of the term 'partner' in modern relationships.

title: After loss and cancer, a Burlington runner keeps moving toward Boston
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Adam Averback is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to honor his late father and his own cancer recovery.
Averback's father received treatment for glioblastoma at Massachusetts General Hospital and passed away in 2020.
After surviving testicular cancer in 2021, Averback began running, transforming it into a therapeutic and motivational journey.

title: New Patriots S Kevin Byard working out with rumored New England trade target A.J. Brown
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: New England Patriots express interest in acquiring Eagles wide receiver A.J. Brown, previously offering a trade that was declined.
Patriots Safety Kevin Byard is working out with A.J. Brown, fueling speculation about a potential trade to New England.
Philadelphia plans to possibly trade Brown after June 1, seeking a first-round draft pick amid ongoing trade rumors.

title: James Hagens watch begins as Bruins brace for top prospect’s potential arrival
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens concluded his sophomore season at Boston College with 47 points over 34 games.
The Boston Bruins anticipate signing Hagens to an entry-level contract to strengthen their roster for the playoff stretch.
Bruins GM Don Sweeney emphasized open discussions regarding Hagens' transition to the team and potential impact.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Peaky Blinders: The Immortal Man' with Cillian Murphy is now streaming on Netflix.
'Born to Bowl' docuseries narrated by Liev Schreiber available on HBO Max.
'Jury Duty: Company Retreat' second season streams on Prime Video featuring hidden camera comedy.
